content.definition: To continue moving forward or to proceed with something.
examples: Go on ahead, I'll catch up later.
content.relatedDefinition: To return to a place you were before.
relatedExamples: I need to go back home, I forgot my keys.
Let's go back to the beginning.
She decided to go back to school.

examples: Come in! The door is open.
What time did you come in last night?
The tide comes in every six hours.
content.relatedDefinition: To leave a place or to be published/released.
relatedExamples: When does the new film come out?
The sun came out after the rain.

content.instructions: Choose the correct phrasal verb to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: Please ___ , the meeting hasn't finished yet.
questions.explanation: 'Go on' means to continue. The sentence is asking someone to continue.

questions.sentence: I forgot my wallet, I need to ___ home.
questions.explanation: 'Go back' means to return to a previous place.
'Come in' means to enter a place.
'Come out' means to appear or emerge.

content.definition: To rise from bed or from a sitting position.
examples: I get up at 7 o'clock every morning.
Get up! You're going to be late!
She got up from her chair and walked to the window.
content.relatedDefinition: To leave a bus, train, or other transport, or to stop touching something.
relatedExamples: We need to get off at the next stop.
What time do you get off work?

content.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb.

questions.sentence: I usually ___ at 6:30 AM on weekdays.
questions.explanation: 'Get up' means to rise from bed.

questions.sentence: Don't forget to ___ at Baker Street station.
questions.explanation: 'Get off' means to leave a form of transport.

questions.sentence: The rain stopped, so let's ___ with our walk.
questions.explanation: 'Go on' means to continue with an activity.

questions.sentence: I left my phone at the office, I'll have to ___.
questions.explanation: 'Go back' means to return to a previous location.

content.instructions: Review all phrasal verbs from this lesson. Match each verb with its meaning.

questions.sentence: The concert will ___ for another two hours.

questions.explanation: 'Come out' means to be released or published.

questions.sentence: He ___ early every morning to go running.
questions.explanation: 'Get up' means to rise from bed.

questions.sentence: We should ___ the bus at the city centre.
